concepts.name: stochastic gradient boosting
concepts.description: A variant of gradient boosting that samples a fraction of training observations at each iteration before growing the next tree, improving performance and computational efficiency.

concepts.name: error-correcting output codes
concepts.description: An ensemble method for multiclass classification that uses a coding matrix to convert a K-class problem into L binary classification problems, where redundant bits allow for inaccuracies and can improve performance.

concepts.name: hamming distance
concepts.description: A distance metric between two vectors measuring the number of mismatches between corresponding entries.

concepts.name: bet on sparsity principle
concepts.description: A principle for high-dimensional problems stating to use procedures that work well in sparse settings, since no procedure does well in dense problems where there are too many nonzero coefficients relative to sample size.

concepts.name: monotone lasso
concepts.description: A variant of the lasso solved by infinitesimal forward stagewise regression, characterized by monotone nondecreasing coefficient paths in an augmented dictionary with non-negative coefficients.

concepts.name: importance sampled learning ensemble
concepts.description: A hybrid ensemble learning approach that generates a finite dictionary of basis functions via randomized sub-sampling and then fits a lasso regularization path to select and weight the most relevant basis functions.

concepts.name: characteristic width of sampling scheme
concepts.description: A measure σ of the randomness in an ensemble generation sampling scheme, defined as the expected increase in loss Q(γ) above the optimal value Q(γ*), balancing between too narrow (similar basis functions) and too wide (many irrelevant cases).

concepts.name: rule ensemble
concepts.description: An ensemble method that extracts individual rules from each tree in a collection, creating an over-complete basis of simpler interpretable components that can be post-processed with lasso or other regularization.

concepts.name: pairwise Markov
concepts.description:
where “rest” refers to all of the other vertices in the graph. For example
in Figure 17.2(a) X ⊥Z|Y . These are known as the pairwise Markov
independencies of G.
If A, B and C are subgraphs, then C is said to separate A and B if every
path between A and B intersects a node in C. For example, Y separates
X and Z in Figures 17.2(a) and (d), and Z separates Y and W in (d). In
Figure 17.2(b) Z is not connected to X, Y, W so we say that the two sets
are separated by the empty set. In Figure 17.2(c), C = {X, Z} separates
Y and W.
Separators have the nice property that they break the graph into con-
ditionally independent pieces. Speciﬁcally, in a Markov graph G with sub-
graphs A, B and C,

concepts.name: clique potentials
concepts.description:
where C is the set of maximal cliques, and the positive functions ψC(·) are
called clique potentials. These are not in general density functions1, but
rather are aﬃnities that capture the dependence in XC by scoring certain
instances xC higher than others. The quantity

concepts.name: partition function
concepts.description:
is the normalizing constant, also known as the partition function. Alterna-
tively, the representation (17.3) implies a graph with independence prop-
erties deﬁned by the cliques in the product. This result holds for Markov
networks G with positive distributions, and is known as the Hammersley-
Cliﬀord theorem (Hammersley and Cliﬀord, 1971; Cliﬀord, 1990).
Many of the methods for estimation and computation on graphs ﬁrst de-
compose the graph into its maximal cliques. Relevant quantities are com-
puted in the individual cliques and then accumulated across the entire
graph. A prominent example is the join tree or junction tree algorithm for
computing marginal and low order probabilities from the joint distribution
on a graph. Details can be found in Pearl (1986), Lauritzen and Spiegel-
halter (1988), Pearl (1988), Shenoy and Shafer (1988), Jensen et al. (1990),
or Koller and Friedman (2007).

concepts.name: graphical lasso
concepts.description:
So up to a factor 1/N, ZT y is the analog of s12, and we replace ZT Z by
W11, the estimated cross-product matrix from our current model.
The resulting procedure is called the graphical lasso, proposed by Fried-
man et al. (2007a) building on the work of Banerjee et al. (2008). It is
summarized in Algorithm 17.2.
Friedman et al. (2007a) use the pathwise coordinate descent method
(Section 3.8.6) to solve the modiﬁed lasso problem at each stage. Here are
the details of pathwise coordinate descent for the graphical lasso algorithm.
Letting V = W11, the update has the form

concepts.name: global markov property
concepts.description: The property in Markov graphs that if subgraph C separates subgraphs A and B, then A and B are conditionally independent given C, allowing decomposition into smaller manageable pieces.

concepts.name: clique in graph theory
concepts.description: A complete subgraph where all vertices are adjacent to one another; a maximal clique is one where no additional vertices can be added while maintaining the property.

concepts.name: gaussian graphical model
concepts.description: A graphical model where all variables are continuous with multivariate Gaussian distribution. The inverse covariance matrix contains information about partial covariances between variables.

concepts.name: natural parameter for gaussian graphical model
concepts.description: The inverse covariance matrix Θ captures all second-order information (structural and quantitative) needed to describe the conditional distribution of each node given the rest.

concepts.name: covariance graph
concepts.description: A graphical model where vertices are connected by bidirectional edges if the covariance (rather than partial covariance) between variables is nonzero. Also called a relevance network.

concepts.name: relevance network
concepts.description: Alternative name for covariance graph, popular in genomics, where edges represent nonzero covariances rather than partial covariances.

concepts.name: iterative proportional fitting procedure
concepts.description: A method for solving equality-constrained convex optimization problems in graphical model estimation where some edges are missing.

concepts.name: meinshausen-bühlmann approach
concepts.description: A simple approach to graphical model estimation that fits lasso regressions using each variable as response and others as predictors, estimating which components of θij are nonzero without fully estimating Σ or Θ.

concepts.name: pathwise coordinate descent for graphical lasso
concepts.description: An efficient algorithm that cycles through predictors, updating each using the soft-threshold operator until convergence, used to solve the modified lasso problem at each stage of graphical lasso.

concepts.name: ising model
concepts.description: An undirected Markov network with all discrete (typically binary) variables modeling pairwise interactions. The joint probability has the form Pr(X) = exp(ΣθjkXjXk - Φ(Θ)).

concepts.name: visible units
concepts.description: Nodes in a Boltzmann machine where the values are observed, as opposed to hidden units.

concepts.name: hidden units in boltzmann machines
concepts.description: Nodes in a Boltzmann machine where the values are unobserved/latent, allowing the model to capture complex nonlinear relationships.

concepts.name: poisson log-linear modeling for graphical models
concepts.description: Treating the Ising model estimation as a regression problem where the response is the vector of 2^p counts in each cell of the multiway tabulation, with computational cost O(p^4*2^p).

concepts.name: mean field approximation
concepts.description: An approximation method that estimates EΘ(XjXk) by EΘ(Xj)EΘ(Xj) and replaces input variables by their means, leading to nonlinear equations for parameters θjk.

concepts.name: recursive feature elimination
concepts.description:
Feature selection is an important scientiﬁc requirement for a classiﬁer when
p is large. Neither discriminant analysis, logistic regression, nor the support-
vector classiﬁer perform feature selection automatically, because all use
quadratic regularization. All features have nonzero weights in both models.
Ad-hoc methods for feature selection have been proposed, for example,
removing genes with small coeﬃcients, and reﬁtting the classiﬁer. This is
done in a backward stepwise manner, starting with the smallest weights and
moving on to larger weights. This is known as recursive feature elimination
(Guyon et al., 2002). It was not successful in this example; Ramaswamy
et al. (2001) report, for example, that the accuracy of the support-vector
classiﬁer starts to degrade as the number of genes is reduced from the full

concepts.name: hard thresholding
concepts.description: An alternative to soft thresholding that sets values to zero below a threshold but leaves values above unchanged, less smooth than soft thresholding.

concepts.name: elastic net penalty
concepts.description: A compromise between lasso and ridge penalties of the form λ[α||β||_1 + (1-α)||β||_2^2], encouraging correlated features to be averaged while maintaining sparsity.

concepts.name: regularized discriminant analysis
concepts.description: A method that shrinks the within-covariance estimate toward its diagonal: ˆΣ(γ) = γˆΣ + (1-γ)diag(ˆΣ), overcoming singularity issues when p >> N.

concepts.name: medoid
concepts.description: The observation in a class with smallest average distance to other observations in that class, used in classification and k-medoids clustering.

concepts.name: junction tree algorithm
concepts.description: An algorithm for computing marginal and low-order probabilities from joint distributions on graphs by decomposing the graph into maximal cliques and accumulating quantities across them, also known as join tree algorithm.

concepts.name: pairwise markov graph
concepts.description: A graphical model with a potential function for each edge (pair of variables) and at most second-order interactions. More parsimonious than general graphical models and easier to work with.

concepts.name: inverse covariance matrix
concepts.description: The matrix Θ = Σ−1 that contains information about partial covariances. If the ijth component is zero, then variables i and j are conditionally independent given other variables.

concepts.name: modified regression algorithm for graphical models
concepts.description: An iterative procedure that solves for Θ and its inverse W one row and column at a time by performing constrained regressions using the current covariance estimate.

concepts.name: positive definite completion
concepts.description: The process of estimating a complete covariance matrix from a partially specified one, subject to constraints from a graphical model structure.

concepts.name: soft-threshold operator
concepts.description: An operator S(x, t) = sign(x)(|x| − t)+ that shrinks values toward zero by thresholding, used in lasso and graphical lasso algorithms.

concepts.name: boltzmann machine
concepts.description: A pairwise Markov network with binary variables where vertices (nodes/units) can be observed (visible) or unobserved (hidden), often organized in layers similar to a neural network.

concepts.name: restricted boltzmann machine
concepts.description: A Boltzmann machine with one layer of visible units and one layer of hidden units with no connections within each layer, simplifying conditional expectation computations.

concepts.name: contrastive divergence
concepts.description: A training method for RBMs where the second expectation in the gradient is estimated by running the Markov chain for only a few steps instead of to convergence, proposed by Hinton (2002).

concepts.name: greedy layer-by-layer learning
concepts.description: A method for learning features in deep networks by training RBMs sequentially, where hidden states from one RBM become visible units for the next.

concepts.name: high-dimensional problem
concepts.description: A prediction problem where the number of features p is much larger than the number of observations N, often written p >> N. High variance and overfitting are major concerns.

concepts.name: diagonal-covariance lda
concepts.description: A version of LDA that assumes features are independent within each class (diagonal within-class covariance matrix), reducing parameters and often working well when p >> N.

concepts.name: independence rule
concepts.description: Alternative name for diagonal-covariance LDA, demonstrated theoretically to often outperform standard LDA in high-dimensional settings.

concepts.name: nearest shrunken centroids
concepts.description: A regularized nearest centroid classifier that shrinks classwise means toward the overall mean for each feature separately using soft thresholding, performing automatic feature selection.

concepts.name: soft thresholding for nearest shrunken centroids
concepts.description: Shrinking standardized classwise differences toward zero using d'_kj = sign(d_kj)(|d_kj| - Δ)+, setting values to zero if their absolute value is less than threshold Δ.

concepts.name: symmetric multiclass logistic model
concepts.description: A version of multiclass logistic regression where all K classes have their own coefficient vectors, with regularization forcing Σˆβ_lj = 0.

concepts.name: one versus one approach
concepts.description: A method for generalizing two-class classifiers to K > 2 classes by computing all pairwise classifiers and using voting to determine the predicted class.

concepts.name: one versus all approach
concepts.description: A method for generalizing two-class classifiers to K > 2 classes by comparing each class to all others in K two-class comparisons, choosing the class with highest confidence.

concepts.name: computational shortcuts for p >> n
concepts.description: Using singular value decomposition to perform computations in N-dimensional space rather than p-dimensional when p > N, reducing cost from O(p^3) to O(pN^2).

concepts.name: protein mass spectrometry
concepts.description: A technology for analyzing proteins in blood by observing intensity for many time of flight values, related to mass over charge ratio (m/z) of constituent proteins.

concepts.name: fused lasso
concepts.description: A modification of the lasso for functional data that penalizes both the L1 norm of coefficients and the L1 norm of their successive differences, encouraging sparsity and smoothness.

concepts.name: fused lasso signal approximator
concepts.description: A special case of fused lasso with X = I_N used to approximate a sequence, balancing fidelity to data with sparsity and smoothness.

concepts.name: string kernel
concepts.description: A similarity measure for variable-length strings based on counting matching substrings, computing inner products K(x1,x2) = Σφ_a(x1)φ_a(x2) where φ_a counts occurrences of substring a.

concepts.name: bag of words representation
concepts.description: A document representation where features x_ij are counts of how many times word j appears in document i, discarding word order.

concepts.name: double-centered kernel
concepts.description: Computing principal components from an uncentered inner-product matrix by centering: (I - M)K(I - M) where M is the mean operator.

concepts.name: supervised principal components
concepts.description: A method for high-dimensional regression that filters features by univariate significance, then computes principal components on the reduced set, and uses them in a regression model.

concepts.name: cox proportional hazards model
concepts.description: A regression model widely used for survival problems, used in supervised principal components for score tests and multivariate modeling with censored outcomes.

concepts.name: right censored survival data
concepts.description: Survival data where for some individuals we only know they lived at least until a certain time, not their exact survival time.

concepts.name: kaplan-meier method
concepts.description: A method for estimating survival curves from censored survival data.

concepts.name: latent variable model for supervised pca
concepts.description: A single-component factor analysis model where outcome Y relates to latent factor U, and relevant features X_j also relate to U through linear models.

concepts.name: thresholded partial least squares
concepts.description: A variant of partial least squares where features are thresholded based on their correlation with the outcome before applying PLS, similar to supervised principal components.

concepts.name: pre-conditioning for interpretability
concepts.description: A method for finding a reduced set of features that approximates a model when the threshold yields too many features for interpretation.

concepts.name: pre-conditioned lasso
concepts.description: A variant of the lasso where the outcome variable is first denoised using supervised principal components, then the lasso is applied with the denoised outcome as the target. All features are used in the lasso fit, not just those retained in thresholding.

concepts.name: two-sample t-statistic for genes
concepts.description: A standardized measure of differential expression between two groups for each gene, computed as the difference in mean expression divided by pooled standard error.

concepts.name: pooled within-group standard error
concepts.description: The standard error estimate for a gene computed by pooling variance estimates across both treatment groups.

concepts.name: permutation distribution for hypothesis testing
concepts.description: A nonparametric approach to computing p-values by permuting sample labels and computing test statistics for each permutation, avoiding distributional assumptions.

concepts.name: pooled permutation p-values
concepts.description: P-values computed by pooling test statistic results across all genes rather than computing separate permutation distributions for each gene, yielding more granular p-values.

concepts.name: family-wise error rate
concepts.description: The probability of making at least one false rejection among all hypothesis tests, a conservative measure of overall error in multiple testing.

concepts.name: bonferroni method
concepts.description: A multiple testing correction that controls family-wise error rate by testing each hypothesis at significance level α/M, where M is the number of tests.

concepts.name: false discovery rate
concepts.description: The expected proportion of false positives among all features called significant, defined as E[V/max(R,1)] where V is false positives and R is total rejections.

concepts.name: benjamini-hochberg procedure
concepts.description: A procedure for controlling false discovery rate at level α by ordering p-values and finding the largest index where p(j) ≤ jα/M, then rejecting all hypotheses with smaller p-values.

concepts.name: plug-in estimate of false discovery rate
concepts.description: A direct estimate of FDR computed as the average number of permutation values exceeding a threshold divided by the number of observed values exceeding that threshold.

concepts.name: significance analysis of microarrays
concepts.description: A method for computing asymmetric significance cutpoints by plotting ordered test statistics against expected order statistics from permutations and finding where points leave a band of width Δ from the 45° line.

concepts.name: asymmetric cutpoints in multiple testing
concepts.description: Using different significance thresholds for positive and negative test statistics when most differentially expressed features change in one direction.

concepts.name: positive false discovery rate
concepts.description: A variant of FDR defined as E[V/R | R > 0], the expected false discovery rate conditional on making at least one rejection.

concepts.name: mixture model for hypothesis testing
concepts.description: A Bayesian framework where each test statistic comes from either the null distribution F0 with probability π0 or the alternative distribution F1 with probability 1-π0.

concepts.name: q-value
concepts.description: The smallest false discovery rate over all rejection regions that would reject a given test statistic, providing a local measure of significance for each feature.

concepts.name: local false discovery rate
concepts.description: The positive FDR for an infinitesimal rejection region surrounding a specific test statistic value, providing the most local measure of significance.

concepts.name: supervised principal components for feature selection
concepts.description: A method that thresholds features based on univariate association with outcome, then performs principal components analysis on retained features and uses components in supervised learning.

concepts.name: denoising the outcome variable
concepts.description: Using a first-stage predictor to reduce noise in the outcome before applying a second-stage method, reducing overfitting with many noisy features.

concepts.name: feature assessment without prediction models
concepts.description: Assessing significance of individual features for scientific understanding rather than building multivariate predictive models, focusing on hypothesis testing.

concepts.name: type-I error rate
concepts.description: The probability of falsely rejecting a true null hypothesis, typically set at α = 0.05 for individual tests.

concepts.name: positive dependence between tests
concepts.description: A correlation structure where Pr(Aj|Ak) > Pr(Aj), meaning tests are more likely to reject together, which reduces family-wise error rate below the independent case.

concepts.name: bh rejection threshold
concepts.description: The p-value cutoff p(L) determined by the Benjamini-Hochberg procedure, where L is the largest index satisfying p(j) ≤ jα/M.

concepts.name: expected order statistics from permutations
concepts.description: The average of ordered test statistics across permutations, denoted ť(j) = (1/K)Σk tk(j), used as null reference in SAM procedure.

concepts.name: sam tuning parameter delta
concepts.description: The width parameter Δ in SAM that defines parallel bands around the 45° line, controlling the distance at which genes are called significant.

concepts.name: bayesian interpretation of fdr
concepts.description: Under a mixture model, the pFDR equals the posterior probability that the null hypothesis is true given the test statistic falls in the rejection region.

concepts.name: radiation sensitivity gene expression study
concepts.description: A microarray study with 58 patient samples (44 normal, 14 radiation-sensitive) and 12,625 genes, used to identify genes associated with adverse radiation response.

concepts.name: diagonal linear discriminant analysis for high dimensions
concepts.description: A variant of LDA that uses a diagonal covariance matrix, appropriate when p >> N and features can be assumed independent.

concepts.name: nearest shrunken centroids classifier
concepts.description: A classification method that shrinks class centroids toward the overall mean, effectively performing feature selection by setting some centroid components to zero.

concepts.name: elastic net
concepts.description: A regularization method combining L1 (lasso) and L2 (ridge) penalties, providing both feature selection and grouping of correlated features.
